title: pneumonanthe viscosa
intro: Pneumonanthe viscosa, also known as sticky lungwort, is a plant species in the balsam family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Pneumonanthe viscosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, syrups, and other medicinal products.
